fields.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-14: future collector/Gemini testing sessions should use Airtable table 'Validation Test Cases' as the durable starting test catalog and update it during runs.
fields.preference_statement: When running future DCOIR collector or Gemini test/evaluation sessions, use the Airtable table 'Validation Test Cases' as the durable starting test catalog rather than rebuilding the test plan from chat memory alone.
fields.effective_behavior: On future collector/Gemini testing requests, first look for and use the Airtable 'Validation Test Cases' table, update observed statuses/evidence during the run, and add/retire test cases as features change.

fields.notes: Imported from dcoir_skill_memory/dcoir-decision-policy/decision_policy_memory.md approved overlay snapshot.
fields.preference_statement: Ask before shifting repo updates to GitHub Desktop or another operator-managed repo-update lane.
fields.effective_behavior: When repo updates could be performed either in chat or through an operator-managed lane such as GitHub Desktop, ask which lane the operator wants before shifting the work.

fields.notes: Updated 2026-05-19 from operator guidance during WBS04.01: exact duplicates are not the only deletion candidates. Same-outcome, stale, superseded, or efficiency-gaining semantic overlaps are merge/removal candidates. Previously completed lanes may be reopened for a more aggressive cleanup pass. Operator authorized aggressive destructive cleanup for the remainder of the plan/WBSs without repeated ask gates, subject to readback, dependency checks, evidence, and Delete Queue discipline.
fields.preference_statement: During DCOIR Airtable cleanup, keep records only when they directly support current DCOIR collector/Gemini deliverables, active governance, operational execution, required validation evidence, or current cleanup execution.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-05-08 operator closeout: durable chatgpt-exec pattern captured after successful WBS22/WBS23 cleanup session; v001 closeout artifact taught that wrapper success is not enough if script-level payload reports failed.
fields.effective_behavior: Use an aggressive operational-retention standard for cleanup, standardization, searchability, filterability, and database usability. Treat rows as merge/removal candidates when they are exact duplicates, near duplicates, superseded, stale, or would lead to essentially the same operational outcome. Merge when efficiency or clarity improves, even if wording differs. Reopen previously completed lanes when a more aggressive pass can better streamline, condense, remove stale material, or advance the cleanup plan. Delete Queue may be used proactively and retrospectively for candidate row deletion after dependency checks. For the remainder of this cleanup plan/WBS work, destructive cleanup actions do not require a fresh ask when the candidate is readback-supported, dependency-safe, and within the current cleanup objective; still preserve evidence, canonical survivor, and pending Delete Queue/readback state.

fields.notes: Imported from operator_intent_matrix.md Rule 8.
fields.preference_statement: Prefer a bounded coordinated campaign over a trickle of one-off pushes when the remaining similar scope is already known.
fields.effective_behavior: When compatible remaining work is already known and regression can stay explicit, prefer one bounded coordinated patch cycle and grouped regression bundle instead of a slow stream of one-off pushes.

fields.notes: Session-scoped workstation context captured during the Gemini bundle source repair branch; do not overstate this as permanent built-in memory outside the project workflow surfaces.
fields.preference_statement: For current-session PowerShell and git delivery in this DCOIR workspace, the operator local dcoir-collector repo path is C:\Users\batch_lrjfue\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\dcoir-collector.
fields.effective_behavior: Use that repo path as the default example repo root in current-session script guidance, and write execution examples assuming the shell is already opened in the script directory so relative-path invocation like .\script.ps1 ... is shown first.

fields.notes: Captured after operator said they do not know how to retrieve a SHA. Applies to CAP workflows and GitHub Desktop/manual bundle steps.
fields.preference_statement: Do not ask the operator to provide a Git commit SHA unless the assistant also gives a simple copy/paste command or tool that retrieves it.
fields.effective_behavior: Prefer GitHub connector readback by expected commit summary, changed files, or latest relevant commit when possible. If a commit SHA is still needed from the operator, provide a PowerShell command such as: git -C $env:DCOIR_REPO_ROOT log -1 --format=%H . Also explain where to paste/run it only when needed.

fields.notes: Updated after operator stated they now delete everything in Downloads after upload/response because prior cleanup behavior was unreliable. Future instructions must package evidence before asking for upload and must not ask follow-up recovery commands that depend on old Downloads run folders unless the operator confirms they still exist.
fields.preference_statement: Use the established DCOIR operator-run PowerShell pattern: create a runner file under DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR, execute it with Tee output, produce uploadable diagnostics, clean up transient ZIP inputs when safe, and include a visible operator handoff/pause when browser UI readiness is required.
fields.effective_behavior: Future local automation code blocks must not run long Playwright/browser steps silently. For Chrome or Airtable UI work, print clear readiness instructions and wait for operator Enter before continuing. Before giving any local cleanup, copy, delete, zip, or recursive file-operation script, explicitly review it for: no self-recursion, no capture-folder self-copy, no unbounded wildcard delete/copy, bounded DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR and DCOIR_REPO_ROOT paths only, final ZIP existence/size verification, clear process-stop instructions, and safe failure behavior. For evidence ZIPs intended for ChatGPT upload, prefer the existing DCOIR packaging surface first: DCOIR-CHATGPT-FRIENDLY-ZIP / operator_tools github_desktop_lane packaging module/script, including Dcoir.Packaging when available, rather than improvised .NET ZipArchive or raw Compress-Archive snippets. Use fallback packaging only if the DCOIR packaging tool/module is unavailable or itself failed, and label the fallback clearly. Assume the operator may delete everything in Downloads after uploading or reporting back; therefore each run must be self-contained and must not depend on prior Downloads artifacts remaining after a turn boundary.

fields.notes: Captured from operator note batch on 2026-04-20. Store as a durable operator-facing Gemini formatting preference for future runtime behavior updates.
fields.preference_statement: When Gemini responds, bold important investigation terms and phrases so analysts can scan faster.
fields.effective_behavior: Prefer visual emphasis for high-signal items such as Elastic close terms, IOCs, hostnames, machine names, usernames, and similarly important entities in the final analyst-facing response.

fields.notes: Derived from repeated transcript guidance in the collector FailureGates repair wave: plain-English step-by-step instructions, exact prompts, right-panel patch review, and web-first safety posture for Codex learning.
fields.preference_statement: For Codex-assisted repo work in DCOIR, prefer plain-English click-by-click instructions, exact prompts to paste, and explicit guidance on which diff/panel/result to bring back.
fields.effective_behavior: When Codex is being used, provide the exact prompt, tell the operator whether to use Copy Patch from the right-hand diff panel, review only the scoped patch before merge, and prefer web/cloud Codex before local-app experimentation unless the operator explicitly wants the local lane.

fields.notes: Tightened after operator caught that wording such as 'like' could let a future session skip valid tables. Rule now requires complete live-schema table inventory classification for each Wave. Current live-schema table universe observed on 2026-05-07: Work Items; Session Checkpoints; Idea Inbox; Plans; Operator Preferences; Validation Test Cases; Queue Control; Gemini Research Reference; Governance Control Plane; Repo Surface Registry; dcoir-memory-preflight; dcoir-decision-policy; dcoir-validation-orchestrator; Delete Queue; Validation Evidence; Admin Registry; DCOIR Lifecycle Ledger; Local Configuration Registry; Operator Tools Registry; DCOIR Cleanup WBS; DCOIR Cleanup Scaffold Registry. Scaffold Registry is classified/deferred to WBS23 unless a Wave explicitly documents a non-disposition readback reason.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR WBS22 cleanup execution, plan every Wave in advance from a complete live-schema table inventory, review what is inside each Wave before execution, and record the reviewed scope so the operator can confirm the Wave covers everything expected.
fields.effective_behavior: Before each WBS22 execution wave, prepare a plain-English target list from live Airtable schema readback. Do not use vague examples such as 'tables like X'. Each Wave plan must classify every live table as included, excluded, or deferred, with a short reason. Then wait for operator feedback or direction. After agreement, update the Wave's WBS record to include the agreed scope, target universe, included tables/fields/record classes, explicitly excluded tables, deferred items, dependencies, approval gate, and readback plan. Do not execute the wave until the reviewed scope is visible and approved when approval is required. Treat a Wave as a grouped database-change lane made of concrete target classes or target surfaces, such as fields, record-update classes, record sets, table restructures, Delete Queue steps, schema/manual changes, or validation/readback targets.

fields.notes: Operator explicitly approved implementing this as a hardened FACT OF LIFE execution rule for generic DCOIR plan lifecycle, including add/update/delete work related to idea-to-plan-to-complete workflow where safe.
fields.preference_statement: DCOIR plans must not be closed until all work items are complete or explicitly skipped, a final checkpoint exists, promotion-readiness state is recorded, and a concise what-changed summary exists.
fields.effective_behavior: Treat plan closure as a required lifecycle gate. Before marking a plan complete, verify work item status, checkpoint state, readiness state, and change summary. If any item is missing, keep the plan active or blocked and create the missing closure artifact.

fields.notes: Related to Operator Tools Registry and dcoir-github-desktop-lane-advisor. Use reusable targeted snapshot helper where possible.
fields.preference_statement: When the operator is at home or says they can do GitHub Desktop pushes, prefer targeted repo snapshots for speed instead of relying on slower connector chunking or broad readback.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-30 operator stated home/GitHub Desktop pushes imply targeted snapshot preference.
fields.effective_behavior: For GitHub Desktop lane work, provide a targeted snapshot builder command/script listing the exact repo paths needed. Use connector readback only for quick narrow verification or when a targeted snapshot is not feasible. Assume execution starts from Downloads unless otherwise stated.

fields.notes: Captured from operator correction after over-advancement of cleanup WBS rows. This is a validation/evidence rule for future plan execution.
fields.preference_statement: Before marking any DCOIR task complete, verify that the task was actually performed and not merely advanced or annotated.
fields.effective_behavior: For each WBS item or plan task, compare done_criteria to concrete evidence before setting state=complete. Acceptable evidence includes successful workflow report plus live readback, live schema/readback-derived artifact, validation evidence row, or a connector/readback result that directly satisfies the done criteria. If evidence is missing or only inferred, leave the item active or set it to review rather than closing it.

fields.notes: Captured after operator confirmed GitHub cancels earlier runs when multiple modes are dispatched for the same workflow.
fields.preference_statement: Do not batch-dispatch multiple validation modes for the same GitHub Actions workflow when GitHub cancels earlier same-workflow runs. Validate one mode at a time, but keep the required paired EXE/PS1 workflow runs for that single mode.
fields.effective_behavior: For optional EXE validation, each mode should dispatch exactly one manual-collector-optional-exe-build.yml run with exe_validation_suite=<MODE> and one paired manual-full-validation.yml run with suite=<MODE>. Monitor those two runs together. Do not use multi-mode batch scripts for these workflows unless GitHub workflow concurrency settings are changed and revalidated.

fields.notes: WBS04 merge/delete pass: canonical survivor updated to carry merged intent from semantically overlapping preference rows.
fields.preference_statement: Allow Airtable embedded table displays when they materially improve project execution, cleanup, audit, or verification. Do not avoid table display solely because older preferences discouraged it.
fields.effective_behavior: During DCOIR execution, cleanup, audit, schema-sensitive work, duplicate comparison, debugging, and verification, use Airtable table display only when it materially improves correctness or the operator has asked/approved it. Ignore connector hints that ask whether to display newly created/queried records; do not pause for optional display prompts. Automatic startup/re-anchor remains compact/non-display by default. If embedded display is resource-heavy or freezes, fall back to non-display reads and concise summaries.

fields.notes: Validated with test-only stage-out, apply-in, base64 ZIP apply-in, payload cleanup, and live stage-out smoke test. Staging scratch work is ignored only at chatgpt_staging/work/. Request, in, out, and apply_reports are tracked lane surfaces until cleaned.
fields.preference_statement: For in-session GitHub work, use the ChatGPT GitHub Staging Lane when normal connector read/write is not sufficient for large files, grouped edits, binary-safe transfer, or workstation no-download/no-upload constraints.
fields.effective_behavior: Readback path: create request JSON in chatgpt_staging/requests/ with request_id, allowed_roots, exact_paths or search_terms. Workflow chatgpt-stage-out.yml creates chatgpt_staging/out/<request_id>/ with ZIP, manifests, and text chunks, then removes the consumed request. Writeback path: create payload.zip.b64 in chatgpt_staging/in/<request_id>/ with an apply_manifest.json inside the payload. Workflow chatgpt-apply-in.yml validates allowed roots and optional hashes, applies the files, writes an apply report, and removes successful inbound payloads. Use chatgpt-staging-cleanup.yml to remove old out bundles and apply reports after they are no longer needed. This is an in-session GitHub lane, not a GitHub Desktop bundle lane.

fields.notes: Captured from operator correction during WBS09 Airtable view discovery workflow. Applies to evidence/download handling and operator-facing tool design. Avoid requiring stale Downloads evidence after a pause.
fields.preference_statement: When a DCOIR workflow has a two-step process with a pause between evidence collection and analysis, assume downloaded evidence may be deleted by the operator immediately after it is provided or uploaded.
fields.effective_behavior: Prefer a singular operator command/tool block that collects, reads/analyzes, summarizes, and packages evidence in one run. Do not depend on prior Downloads folders or ZIPs surviving between steps unless the operator explicitly says they kept them.

fields.notes: Updated from narrower at-work condition to broader operator correction: use this lane whenever the operator is unable to execute locally at all, no matter where they are. Original captured approval: "I approve but only when I am unable to do the executions myself due to being at work." Follow-up correction: "it doesn't have to be specifically when I'm at work it can just be when I'm unable to at all no matter where I'm at."
fields.preference_statement: For AFRICOM_SOC_IR / DCOIR sessions, prefer ChatGPT-staged GitHub Actions, chatgpt-exec/apply-in, and reusable repo operator tools when the operator is unable to execute locally, regardless of location or reason.
fields.effective_behavior: When this condition applies, proceed autonomously through GitHub/action/operator-tool lanes for non-destructive evidence collection, readback, workflow execution, and repo-supported automation without pausing for routine status-only updates. Do not treat this as a blanket preference when the operator can execute locally. Airtable cleanup writes, deletes, merges, schema changes, Delete Queue processing, skill changes, and production source changes still require explicit approval unless separately authorized.

fields.notes: Captured from operator analyst note after T2 artifact review. This is a validation-process preference, not a one-off for the current T2 branch only.
fields.preference_statement: Apply the same level of deep post-bundle review thoroughness to T1-only collector runs as is being applied to T2 during this massive validation wave, including analyst-machine inventory, spot-checks of meaningful artifacts, and review of whether higher-level summaries stay honest about degraded lanes.

fields.notes: Imported from operator_intent_matrix.md Rule 3.
fields.preference_statement: Default to deeper regression for anything testable before live use and after every patch.
fields.effective_behavior: Choose deeper regression rather than minimal smoke coverage when reliable testing is available, and rerun that deeper regression after every patch before treating the artifact as ready again.

fields.notes:
Created during 2026-05-03 project-health recovery from operator feedback that a prior bundle contained bloat file CHATGPT_BUNDLE_COMMIT_SUMMARY.md.

MERGE 2026-05-19: Confirmed as canonical broader repo-bundle no-bloat preference. Supersedes rec6HD2ZVKgIjDEt4 / BUNDLE-NO-CHATGPT-ONLY-ARTIFACTS while preserving its provenance about GitHub Desktop bundles and ChatGPT-only helper artifacts.
fields.preference_statement: Never include ChatGPT-only or operator-note artifacts inside repo-update bundles, including CHATGPT_BUNDLE_COMMIT_SUMMARY.md. Bundle only affected repo-relative files.
fields.effective_behavior: For GitHub Desktop repo-update bundles, provide commit messages, bundle notes, and operator instructions in chat only. Do not include wrapper roots, chat-only summary files, logs, or unrelated files in the ZIP.

fields.notes: Updated 2026-05-08 after operator correction: malformed apply-in bundles were assistant generation/verification errors, not connector failures. New rule requires meticulous local bundle generation and verification before staging, with exact hash/length/readback evidence.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR cleanup waves and workflow execution lanes, require real wave goals before execution, before/after improvement metrics, visible progress over time, and burden reduction where wave scope permits. Prefer chatgpt-exec for bulk operations to reduce browser/Airtable UI burden. Non-destructive discovery, classification, before/after metrics collection, proposal generation, readback, evidence review, diagnostic staging, and direct GitHub connector repair of workflow/readback gaps may proceed autonomously when inside the current wave scope or when repairing the execution/readback lane.
fields.effective_behavior: Before staging chatgpt-apply-in payload.zip.b64, generate the ZIP/base64 bundle using a real tool or local script, not manual/pasted construction. Verify ZIP open/CRC, apply_manifest.json and files/ root shape, base64 length divisible by 4, base64 decode round-trip, ZIP SHA256, payload.zip.b64 SHA256, manifest target hashes, and no truncation or non-base64 characters. Do not blame connector/tooling for malformed payloads until local generation and post-stage readback evidence proves the connector changed a verified payload. For repo patch batches, prefer one carefully verified apply-in bundle; use at most two bundles only when size/scope requires it.

fields.notes: Operator explicitly approved the 2026-04-26 closeout starter prompt as the desired future standard: more verbose than prior starter prompts, detailed enough to prevent next-session drift, and grounded in exact Airtable/GitHub state. Treat this as a durable operator preference for DCOIR project closeouts and resume prompts.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR / AFRICOM_SOC_IR closeouts, starter prompts must be verbose, explicit, and detailed enough to resume without drifting. The 2026-04-26 T4.0.5.6 closeout starter prompt is the benchmark style and level of thoroughness.
fields.effective_behavior: When producing future starter prompts or session handoffs, include the exact startup sequence, current plan, exact active task/branch, completed-and-verified items, blocked/open items, explicit do-not-resume/do-not-proceed boundaries, deepest known task path, next required execution path, operator preferences, governance/safety rules, known checkpoints, and one best next move. Avoid terse starter prompts that rely on prior chat memory. Make the starter prompt self-contained enough that a new session can bootstrap and continue at the correct Airtable task without reopening completed branches or jumping ahead.

fields.notes: WBS04 merge/delete pass: canonical survivor updated to carry merged intent from semantically overlapping preference rows.
fields.preference_statement: Generated DCOIR code and operator-run codeblocks must use Local Configuration Registry for known local/system configuration names and safe runtime references instead of inventing variable names, hard-coding values, or asking again when a confirmed registry row exists.
fields.effective_behavior: Before emitting DCOIR operator-run codeblocks or setup instructions that need local configuration, consult Local Configuration Registry for canonical environment variable names and safe references. Use registered references, fail fast with missing variable names, avoid printing values unless safe_to_display is true and display is necessary, and never print/log/package secret, token, API key, or project identifier values.

fields.notes: Operator said this should persist across sessions until told otherwise, and gave Airtable Omni AI and Codex/GitHub examples.
fields.preference_statement: When a DCOIR workflow requires manual operator action and an AI tool is available, provide an AI-ready prompt for that tool.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-29 operator instruction after final cleanup v4 appeared hung
fields.effective_behavior: Whenever instructions require the operator to do something manually, include a ready-to-paste AI prompt for the appropriate assistant/tool when available, such as Airtable Omni AI for Airtable UI cleanup or Codex/GitHub tooling for GitHub changes. The prompt should be specific, bounded, include safety checks, and tell the AI tool what to verify and what not to delete/change. Still include plain-English manual steps where needed.

fields.notes: This preference expands scripting-package generation beyond PowerShell. Use Python when it improves JSON/schema handling, CSV generation, ZIP creation, validation, or API correctness.
fields.preference_statement: DCOIR scripting packages should use the best local toolchain for precision and reliability, not PowerShell by default when another tool is better.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-28: operator explicitly stated Python is installed and other CLIs can be installed if they improve precision.
fields.effective_behavior: For Airtable, GitHub, and Project packages, choose the safest fit among Windows PowerShell 5.1, Python, CMD wrappers, GitHub CLI, Airtable/API tooling, or other installable CLIs. Still provide a zip package with simple numbered entrypoints or clear command-line execution, and keep instructions in the chat in plain English. Ask for new environment variables only when needed and avoid exposing secrets in ChatGPT.

fields.notes: Created 2026-05-05 after operator corrected assistant for not persisting the recurring preference to verify markers after install confirmation. Scope: DCOIR skill maintenance/update flows.
fields.preference_statement: After the operator confirms a DCOIR skill install, immediately verify installed-skill readback for the expected skill marker and added reference files before proceeding to the next skill or task.
fields.effective_behavior: For DCOIR skill update flows, do not treat install confirmation as sufficient. After each operator install confirmation, read the installed skill via api_tool_skills, confirm the expected skill-marker in SKILL.md, confirm any added reference files/resources are present, report marker/readback status, and only then continue. If marker/readback fails or lags, stop bounded progress and report installed-skill drift or readback lag.

fields.notes:
Candidate preference only. Use after explicit operator setup and when task benefit outweighs added complexity.
CURATED 2026-04-29T05:41:18+00:00: Contains operator/approved-rule/mandatory behavior language; promote out of candidate state. Original key=PREF-DCOIR-OPTIONAL-OPENAI-API-LOCAL-AI-20260428
fields.preference_statement: When useful, DCOIR local scripting packages may optionally integrate ChatGPT/OpenAI API assistance, provided the operator stores the API key locally and scripts do not expose it in chat or artifacts.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-28: operator offered to save an API key as an environment variable if useful.
fields.curation_reason: Contains operator/approved-rule/mandatory behavior language; promote out of candidate state.
fields.effective_behavior: If AI-assisted local scripts would materially improve precision, propose a local environment variable or config-file secret pattern before use. Do not hardcode API keys. Do not require AI integration for deterministic tasks that can be handled safely without it. Keep generated outputs reviewable through dry-run and verification artifact zips.

fields.notes: Created during PLAN-20260503-project-health-recovery after operator clarified that DCOIR is building a reusable pseudo-object-oriented toolbox across PowerShell, Python, and future technologies.
fields.preference_statement: Before creating, modifying, or recommending DCOIR operator-side tool code, always check the existing reusable toolbox first for parts and patterns to reuse.
fields.effective_behavior: For PowerShell, Python, and future operator-tool technologies, first inspect Operator Tools Registry, repo tool catalogs, existing modules, scripts, docs, and related helper skills. Prefer extending or composing existing reusable modules over rediscovering implementation patterns or creating standalone one-off tools. Treat the toolbox as a pseudo object-oriented library of reusable capabilities.

fields.notes: Operator provided explicit examples and clarified that the same strategy applies to entire tables, field merges, table merges, and nearly any schema/data movement. This is the expected aggressive Airtable migration model for future DCOIR packages.
fields.preference_statement: For Airtable field-type conversion or field merge work, use a staged replacement pattern: rename the old field with a DELETE - prefix, create or use a new correctly typed field with the original intended name, copy/format data into the typed field, then clear data from the old DELETE-prefixed field after verification.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-29 operator instruction during Airtable aggressive cleanup evidence review
fields.effective_behavior: Preferred staged replacement pattern: 1) rename old field/table to 'DELETE - <name>'; 2) create or promote a replacement field/table with the original intended name and correct Airtable type/shape; 3) copy, format, normalize, and verify data into the replacement; 4) after verification, clear old field data or mark old table rows for retirement; 5) only later delete the DELETE-prefixed object after dependency review. Apply this not only to single fields, but also to field merges, table merges, replacement tables, and broader schema/data-type migrations. Do not leave duplicate active authorities when a staged conversion can safely finish the migration.

fields.notes: Operator uploaded '0846 DCOIR_Airtable_Table_Merge_And_Retirement_Evidence.zip' and instructed that future script-output re-upload bundles must be prefixed as YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS - [REST OF THE BUNDLE NAME] to avoid wrong-bundle extraction/review.
fields.preference_statement: When DCOIR script packages output a bundle that the operator will re-upload to ChatGPT, prefix the output filename with a sortable timestamp in the form 'YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS - '.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-29 operator instruction after table merge evidence upload
fields.effective_behavior: For generated review/evidence/artifact ZIPs and similar re-upload bundles, use filenames like '20260429T084600 - DCOIR_Airtable_Table_Merge_And_Retirement_Evidence.zip'. This reduces the risk of ChatGPT extracting or reviewing the wrong similarly named artifact. Apply this to future script packages and artifact collectors.

fields.notes: Updated after operator hit HTTP 422 because manual-full-validation.yml input is suite, not validation_suite.
fields.preference_statement: When validating the optional collector EXE workflow with a selected suite or mode, also run the PS1 collector validation through manual-full-validation with the matching suite/mode so EXE-specific fixes do not break the primary PowerShell collector lane.
fields.effective_behavior: For any future optional EXE validation dispatch, pair manual-collector-optional-exe-build.yml with manual-full-validation.yml using the closest matching suite/mode. Current input names: manual-collector-optional-exe-build.yml uses exe_validation_suite; manual-full-validation.yml uses suite. Example pair for Core: gh workflow run manual-collector-optional-exe-build.yml -R malwaredevil/dcoir-collector --ref main -f exe_validation_suite=Core; gh workflow run manual-full-validation.yml -R malwaredevil/dcoir-collector --ref main -f suite=Core. Monitor both runs together and compare pass/fail evidence before accepting the EXE lane. If one workflow supports a mode that the other does not, select the nearest available equivalent and state the mismatch explicitly.

fields.notes: Imported from dcoir-decision-policy/references/operator_intent_matrix.md Rule 1.
fields.preference_statement: Minimize avoidable operator maintenance overhead.
fields.effective_behavior: Prefer the lower-friction design that preserves control and auditability without adding recurring manual maintenance work.

fields.notes: Updated 2026-05-09 from operator clarification during PLAN-AIRTABLE-DB-REDESIGN-20260508: prior approval to use/create operator tools applies for the rest of the plan and post-plan future, including Airtable Scripting Extension, Airtable automations, all Airtable-native capabilities, and tools developed using any suitable technology. This reduces the need for the operator to restate available execution lanes while preserving approval gates for destructive/high-impact actions.
fields.preference_statement: Use the strongest appropriate execution technologies for DCOIR work, including Windows Subsystem for Linux, Docker Desktop, PowerShell, Python, Rust or other programming languages, ChatGPT connectors, skills, Airtable Scripting Extension, Airtable automations and other Airtable-native capabilities, free online services, APIs, Personal Access Tokens, and reusable operator tools when they materially improve effectiveness, reliability, validation, automation, or operator burden reduction.
fields.effective_behavior: During planning and execution, consider WSL, Docker Desktop, programming languages, connectors, helper skills, Airtable Scripting Extension, Airtable automations, Airtable native features, online services, APIs, PATs, chatgpt-exec, and reusable operator tools as available operator-approved options. Prefer existing operator_tools first; create or extend durable reusable tools when existing tooling is insufficient and the work is within the approved overall goal. Request credentials, setup, manual Airtable UI steps, or local runtime preparation only when actually needed. Continue to preserve safety, authority, least-necessary credential use, exact lane separation, and destructive/high-impact approval gates.

fields.notes: Captured from operator correction during DCOIR Airtable Cleanup session: do not assume a connector block is caused by blocked words/phrases; after-action review often shows typo, misspelling, or malformed request shape. Review own work first before attributing to platform safety filtering.
fields.preference_statement: For AFRICOM_SOC_IR / DCOIR work, when a connector or platform call is blocked or rejected, first review the exact request shape, spelling, field names, API arguments, and syntax before attributing the issue to a safety filter or platform block.
fields.effective_behavior: On connector/tool failures, classify the failure as malformed-or-blocked until checked. Inspect obvious typos, misspellings, wrong tool names, unsupported fields, bad filters, field-id/table-id mismatches, and request-shape errors first. Only report a safety/platform block after the request has been reviewed and still appears correctly formed. Continue work through the smallest safe retry or alternate lane where possible.

fields.notes: Captured from operator instruction during Actions orchestrator gate validation: 'in the future after you show the pull latest from repo, go ahead and show the execute code block to save on round trip responses'.
fields.preference_statement: When showing a 'pull latest' command for a repo during DCOIR operator-tool workflows, also include the next executable command block in the same response to avoid a follow-up round trip.
fields.effective_behavior: After instructing the operator to pull latest, immediately provide the next run/execute PowerShell block unless it would be unsafe or depends on unseen output from the pull. Keep both blocks easy to copy.

fields.notes: Added from operator instruction in the observation-log session so future Gemini-related observations are routed to the Gemini plan by default.
fields.preference_statement: Anything Gemini-related that the operator mentions, regardless of where it is stored in Airtable, should be fixed during the Gemini-related plan unless explicitly overridden.
fields.effective_behavior: Route Gemini-related fixes, remediation notes, instruction changes, and observation-driven follow-up work into the Gemini remediation plan instead of pulling them into unrelated active plans. This applies even when the originating observation is stored in Idea Inbox or another Airtable surface.

fields.notes: Existing Validation Test Cases schema already supports this through the multiline Evidence / Notes field, so no table-structure change is required right now. Apply this rule to prior and future rows in the current wave.
fields.preference_statement: When saving validation results to Airtable, include contextual notes and any produced error messages in the Validation Test Cases record so later fix/review work starts with enough evidence.

fields.notes: Created after all 15 current Airtable tables were verified to have delete_requested and table-specific delete automations validated. This is a durable operator preference/workflow guardrail, not a replacement for schema review.
fields.preference_statement: Whenever a new Airtable table is created for the AFRICOM_SOC_IR / DCOIR workspace, it must include a delete_requested checkbox field and receive its own per-table delete_requested automation before being treated as fully operational.
fields.effective_behavior: For every new DCOIR Airtable table, add a delete_requested checkbox field, duplicate the last validated per-table delete automation, retarget the trigger and script to the new table, validate with one scratch row only, and never create a broad/base-wide delete automation.

fields.notes: This should shape future Airtable and GitHub package generation. 02 should collect/review artifacts where practical; 05 remains final evidence collection.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR scripting packages, the operator should upload dry-run and verification artifacts before applying state-changing changes when the package affects Airtable schema, GitHub repo state, or project governance surfaces.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-28: captured after operator explicitly requested durable dry-run upload behavior.
fields.effective_behavior: Design packages so 02_DryRun or equivalent produces a zipped review bundle, not just loose files, when the dry run caps a verification/review step. Ask for or expect upload of the dry-run ZIP before advising the operator to apply changes. After apply, still require a post-apply verification/readback bundle.

fields.notes: Captured during T4.0 Airtable delete_requested automation verification.
fields.preference_statement: For Airtable delete_requested automations, use the naming pattern "Delete <Table Name> record on delete_requested" and only provide the table-line snippet when the rest of the automation code is unchanged.
fields.effective_behavior: When guiding new delete_requested automations, keep instructions brief after the first setup: remind the operator to select delete_requested and recordId, then provide only the table-specific line such as let table = base.getTable("Repo File Coverage Detail"); unless the full script changes.

fields.notes: Imported from decision_policy_memory.md approved overlay snapshot.
fields.preference_statement: For GitHub existing-file or grouped existing-file friction, perform validated recovery before writing durable capability-boundary claims.
fields.effective_behavior: For GitHub-family existing-file or grouped existing-file friction, re-anchor and apply the canonical recovery procedures before writing durable limitation or connector-failure claims.

fields.notes: Updated 2026-05-19 after operator instruction: proceed using "semi" autonomous operations until told otherwise. This broadens the prior autonomous green/yellow preference while preserving explicit hard stops for destructive, authority-changing, broad schema, source, production, or Delete Queue actions.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR work, proceed semi-autonomously inside already-defined planning, validation, cleanup, or approval-packet scope until the operator says otherwise. If the operator replies k after an exact approval packet or recommendation, treat k as approval of that immediately preceding exact packet and agreement to proceed inside that stated scope.
fields.effective_behavior: Use semi-autonomous mode for green/yellow-zone DCOIR work: perform non-destructive discovery, schema/readback, validation, proposal generation, checkpointing, evidence review, and low-risk helper-only execution inside the current plan scope without pausing for routine status-only approvals. Stop or ask before destructive actions, table deletion, broad schema changes, primary-field replacement, record deletion, Delete Queue processing, production/source changes, or anything that changes authority/safety/release scope. Use k only for the most recent exact approval packet or recommendation and do not expand scope beyond that packet. For multiple approved Airtable records, prefer grouped or batch create/update operations rather than one-record-at-a-time work. Use direct Airtable batch calls when adequate; use an operator-tool or chatgpt-exec style manifest lane for larger repeatable batches or when a scripted lane is safer.

fields.notes: Updated during T6G after operator observed chatgpt-apply-in succeeded but costs workflow initialization plus about 18 seconds and asked to minimize GitHub commits by installing/validating a few skills before a grouped GitHub apply-in where practical.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR skill patch/create work in this session, package and install one skill at a time, wait for operator install/update plus marker verification before moving to the next skill, and minimize GitHub commits by batching verified skill-source updates into grouped chatgpt-apply-in source syncs when practical.
fields.effective_behavior: Strict workflow: handle installable skill packages one skill at a time. Operator installs/updates the single skill in the skills market/editor and reports the marker. Assistant verifies marker/readback when available. Do not immediately push every verified skill to GitHub if more compatible skill installs or repo doc/rule edits are likely in the same branch; instead buffer source-sync items and use one grouped chatgpt-apply-in transaction at a stable checkpoint, unless an intermediate GitHub commit is needed for safety, dependency ordering, or operator handoff. For skills to delete, operator deletes them in the marketplace. Use GitHub source sync only through chatgpt-apply-in unless operator explicitly changes lane.

fields.notes: Captured during DB redesign plan execution after the operator requested a plan-specific rule. This is plan-specific first; do not generalize project-wide unless proven useful across the plan. Include this check in starter prompts, continuity prompts between WBS tasks/waves, and research planning where an expert would reduce ambiguity or improve outcomes.
fields.preference_statement: For PLAN-AIRTABLE-DB-REDESIGN-20260508, before starting a new WBS task, wave, approval packet, research pass, migration step, schema change, destructive/high-impact action, or starter/continuity prompt, evaluate whether the current inherited expertise is sufficient. If additional domain expertise would materially improve correctness, safety, design quality, validation, or operational usefulness, generate an explicit expertise-inheritance prompt for the operator to execute before proceeding.
fields.effective_behavior: Apply only when the current expertise is insufficient or could be materially improved. Do not spam expertise prompts for routine low-risk steps. Use the WBS and next task context to anticipate needed expert lenses such as Airtable database architect, data normalization specialist, controlled-vocabulary designer, workflow/governance designer, safe migration planner, validation engineer, schema dependency analyst, DCOIR collector/Gemini deliverable specialist, UX/search/filter designer, and records-retention/cleanup reviewer. The prompt should be actionable, task-specific, and scoped to the next WBS/wave rather than generic.

fields.notes: Captured from operator note batch on 2026-04-20. This is both an execution preference and a cleanup safety constraint.
fields.preference_statement: If GitHub surfaces duplicative of Airtable live control are later removed, the cleanup should be manual by the operator with a local backup retained first.
fields.effective_behavior: Do not perform assistant-side delete execution for duplicate-control cleanup by default. Build the inventory first, let the operator keep a local backup, and treat rollback as a local/operator path if needed.

fields.notes: Captured after operator correction during DCOIR core skill source parity sync. This preference prevents recurrence of the partial-parts staging error and reinforces the runbook lane.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR chatgpt-apply-in staging, use the proven single payload.zip.b64 file path rather than creating payload.zip.b64.parts or chunk marker files.
fields.effective_behavior: When staging chatgpt-apply-in payloads, build one validated ZIP with apply_manifest.json at archive root, base64-encode it as one file, and write only chatgpt_staging/in/<request_id>/payload.zip.b64 unless the operator explicitly requests a different method. Do not introduce parts mode or chunk files as a workaround.

fields.notes: Captured from operator correction during WBS09 Airtable native view automation: hidden temp run folders are unacceptable because they hide clutter rather than solve it. Pair with OPREF-DOWNLOADS-EVIDENCE-ONE-SHOT-20260510: use one-shot workflows but keep outputs in governed visible paths.
fields.preference_statement: Do not create DCOIR generated files, evidence, run folders, logs, bundles, or intermediate artifacts outside operator-provided paths stored in governed environment variables such as DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR, unless the operator explicitly approves a different path in that turn.
fields.effective_behavior: Use visible governed output paths for all generated artifacts. Do not use hidden temp locations as a clutter workaround. If a previous run created DCOIR artifacts under temp or another non-governed path, provide a cleanup command for the exact DCOIR-created folders before continuing.

fields.notes: Captured from live collector validation session after operator explicitly affirmed the command-first workflow and the need to narrow commands when larger outputs could make the chat laggy.
fields.preference_statement: For analyst-machine bundle review in DCOIR validation, prefer one exact command at a time with the operator pasting the command output back into chat instead of asking the operator to open many individual files manually.
fields.effective_behavior: Use command-first Windows-native review on the analyst workstation when feasible: start with cmd.exe inventory commands and then use bounded type/findstr style commands to print only the needed review surfaces back into chat. When file size or output volume would risk lag or jam the session, switch to narrower commands or smaller excerpts rather than dumping large files wholesale.

fields.notes: Captured from operator statement on 2026-05-09 during WBS02 table/surface taxonomy branch: related multi-step approvals are acceptable to reduce unnecessary pauses. This complements but does not override exact approval gates for destructive or high-impact work.
fields.preference_statement: The operator can approve multiple related DCOIR steps under one overall goal so the assistant does not need to stop for each individual packet or action.
fields.effective_behavior: When the operator gives a broad but related approval, proceed through the included bounded steps without pausing after every prepared packet, as long as each action stays within the approved overall goal and does not cross into a materially different safety, schema, deletion, GitHub source-change, workflow, or authority class. Continue to stop for destructive/high-impact changes, scope changes, or unclear authority.

fields.notes: Imported from operator_intent_matrix.md Rule 7.
fields.preference_statement: Treat delivery and packaging preferences as durable passive-capture candidates.
fields.effective_behavior: When the operator states a preference about bundle shape, download count, update handoff, or similar delivery friction, classify it through passive preference capture and preserve it as durable working state instead of relying on chat-only memory.

fields.notes: WBS04 merge/delete pass: canonical survivor updated to carry merged intent from semantically overlapping preference rows.
fields.preference_statement: When developing DCOIR operator tools, put reusable behavior in parameterized shared modules rather than embedding durable logic in one-off harnesses or WBS-specific scripts.
fields.effective_behavior: Put reusable behavior in parameterized shared modules/tools; keep harnesses thin for parameters, launch, logging, output folders, and evidence packaging. Before creating or extending tools, check Operator Tools Registry, repo tool catalogs, existing modules/scripts/docs, and related helper skills. Repair durable tools before creating one-off alternates; promote reusable chatgpt-exec/operator-tool patterns into operator_tools when they recur.

fields.notes: WBS04 merge/delete pass: canonical survivor updated to carry merged intent from semantically overlapping preference rows.
fields.preference_statement: User prefers short, brief, plain-English responses with minimal technical jargon and simple structure.
fields.effective_behavior: Use concise plain-English summaries with minimal jargon. For substantive DCOIR status/progress/cleanup/closeout responses, include the verified state, caveats/blockers, and the best next move when useful. Include near-equivalent alternatives only when they materially help decision-making. Avoid walls of text and routine status-only pauses during approved execution campaigns.

fields.notes: Operator confirmed the final cleanup/replacement method after T99 was added as late-plan cleanup branch.
fields.preference_statement: For final DCOIR repo end-state replacement, preserve the local .git folder and replace only the working-tree contents with the assistant-provided end-state bundle.
fields.effective_behavior: When building final repo replacement guidance or bundles, package only final repository contents with no wrapper root and no .git directory; instruct the operator to keep .git locally, delete/replace the rest of the working tree, then commit and push through GitHub Desktop.

fields.notes: Imported from operator_intent_matrix.md Rule 5.
fields.preference_statement: Default to exhaustive documentation and continuity-first knowledge capture when richer detail materially helps continuity or maintenance.
fields.effective_behavior: Preserve materially useful evidence, provenance, rationale, assumptions, validation notes, and downstream implications instead of compressing them away when future continuity matters.

fields.notes: Captured at closeout per operator request. This complements existing preferences to ignore Airtable display hints, avoid workflow-disruptive prompts, prefer modular/general-use tooling, and use GitHub Desktop/patch bundles for multi-file refactors. Avoid duplicate prompts or display asks.
fields.preference_statement: For WBS09 Airtable UI automation batches, operator-facing PowerShell codeblocks should keep work in one lane: start with safe cleanup of superseded WBS09 Downloads artifacts, run the bounded batch, automatically create a ChatGPT-friendly ZIP of the run output on success, and end with a Downloads inventory. On failure, give specific inspection steps: keep browser open if available, take a screenshot, press Enter to close/end the tool session only after evidence is captured, then run a ZIP command for the failure output folder.
fields.effective_behavior: When generating future WBS09 batch execution blocks, include cleanup at the beginning, run validation/node checks and the bounded batch, create/upload-ready .chatgpt.zip artifacts for successful output folders, include failure ZIP instructions tied to the latest output folder, and include a final Downloads inventory so the next assistant turn can provide a precise deletion block without starting a second work lane.

fields.notes: Captured after the skill-bundle install branch when the operator restated the desire to make the in-session skill update path durable before collector hardening.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR skill maintenance, prefer an in-session update path when safe: verify the installed skill update, then carry the GitHub source, parity, and related follow-through in the same working branch before falling back to manual bundle-only handling.
fields.effective_behavior: Prefer the chat-driven update path for skill repairs and refreshes when the lane is safe and the installed result can be verified. Use manual GitHub Desktop or manual install bundles as fallback lanes, not the default, unless the operator explicitly chooses them or the in-session lane is blocked.

fields.notes: Captured from the Gemini baseline-copy repair branch after operator-confirmed local PowerShell execution.
fields.preference_statement: PowerShell plus git is an approved alternate DCOIR delivery method alongside in-session and GitHub Desktop delivery when direct local repo manipulation is the better fit.
fields.effective_behavior: When a ZIP is unnecessary, provide repo-safe PowerShell scripts intended to be run from the current working directory using relative-path execution such as .\script.ps1 ..., and include git-aware guidance when it helps review or commit the local repo changes.

fields.notes: Imported from operator_intent_matrix.md Rule 10.
fields.preference_statement: In operator-approved coordinated campaigns, continue executing until there is a real action, blocker, or decision.
fields.effective_behavior: Do not stop at intermediate milestones during an approved campaign unless there is a real GitHub step, skill-install step, blocker, materially changed evidence state, or decision that genuinely requires operator input.

fields.notes: Captured after operator reported failed download status for the zipped GitHub Actions monitor package and asked to avoid unnecessary ZIP/README overhead for one-file scripts.
fields.preference_statement: When delivering a simple operator-run script that is a single file and does not require supporting assets, provide the script file directly instead of wrapping it in a ZIP. Do not include a README for these simple script-file deliveries unless the script is complex enough to need one.
fields.effective_behavior: Prefer direct .ps1, .cmd, .py, or similar files for single-file utility scripts. Continue to use ZIPs for multi-file packages, bundles that must preserve repo-relative paths, GitHub Desktop patch bundles, or deliverables with multiple artifacts. Keep inline instructions concise in chat rather than adding README overhead for simple scripts.

fields.notes: WBS04 merge/delete pass: canonical survivor updated to carry merged intent from semantically overlapping preference rows.
fields.preference_statement: For GitHub Actions validation, prefer a single operator-run monitor script over multiple one-off gh commands when practical. The script should show visible progress, wait for selected runs to complete, write upload-ready logs/results/artifacts only after completion, and place outputs in DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR with YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS_ prefixes.
fields.effective_behavior: For GitHub Actions monitoring, prefer a reusable operator-run monitor script when it reduces copy/paste friction. Put all output for one monitoring run in one timestamp-prefixed folder under DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR, show visible console progress, treat no-artifacts-found as nonfatal unless artifacts are the pass condition, and create text-only upload ZIPs by default that include logs/summaries/JSON/YAML/Markdown/scripts but exclude binaries, nested ZIPs, DLLs, images, Office/PDF artifacts unless specifically requested.

fields.notes: WBS04 merge/delete pass: canonical survivor updated to carry merged intent from semantically overlapping preference rows.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR chatgpt-exec and similar GitHub workflow executions, use Gmail GitHub notifications as a broad liveness signal in combination with request-specific heartbeat/readback files. Do not start, restage, or update a workflow request to retrigger execution until the previous request has a definitive terminal status or status is declared unknowable and stopped.
fields.effective_behavior: Use Gmail Label_125 with repo subject prefix [malwaredevil/dcoir-collector] as a supplementary liveness/sequencing stream and request-scoped workflow_report.md/final_readback_marker/GitHub run status as evidence authority. Poll by request_id and do not duplicate/retrigger a request until terminal status or an explicit unknowable stop. Do not surface every routine polling read during approved cleanup/execution campaigns; surface completed results, true blockers, material evidence changes, or operator-required decisions.

fields.notes: Captured during staging-lane cleanup automation design. Applies directly to potential updates for dcoir-memory-preflight, dcoir-decision-policy, dcoir-github-desktop-lane-advisor, and dcoir-validation-orchestrator. Goal: allow future behavior tweaks by updating Airtable rather than repackaging skills whenever safe.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR helper skills that provide action recommendations, lane choices, routing, or workflow behavior, avoid hardcoding detailed policy into the skill when an Airtable-controlled row can carry the changeable behavior.
fields.effective_behavior: Design skills as Airtable-aware readers/enforcers: skill instructions should define the lookup contract, safety boundaries, and fallback behavior, while tweakable routing rules, lane preferences, recommendation matrices, and operational defaults should live in Airtable helper-memory, SKILLROUTE, Operator Preferences, or other appropriate governance tables.

fields.notes: Operator explicitly said: 'For tables that are obsolete after these more agressive moves, prefix there names with DELETE -'. This is part of the architecture migration retirement rule.
fields.preference_statement: When Airtable tables become obsolete after reviewed architecture migration moves, rename them with the prefix 'DELETE - ' instead of immediately deleting them.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-28 operator instruction during Airtable architecture side path
fields.effective_behavior: For obsolete Airtable tables, use a staged-retirement pattern: verify replacement authority, move or backfill needed data, update dependent scripts/skills/references, then rename the obsolete table to 'DELETE - <Original Name>' before any later deletion decision. Do not rename tables that are still active authority or dependency targets.

fields.notes: Operator clarified and approved strict interpretation: invoke every currently installed dcoir-* skill before every DCOIR response, with no applicability-only exception. Project Instructions and attachments should be updated to remove ambiguity. This preference supersedes softer wording such as 'invoke relevant/applicable skills' for DCOIR response gates.
fields.preference_statement: For AFRICOM_SOC_IR / DCOIR sessions, the assistant must answer and operate from expert/master-level competence in the approved DCOIR expertise baseline and must invoke every currently installed dcoir-* skill before every DCOIR response. This is not an applicability-only rule: every installed dcoir-* skill must be invoked in compact awareness/gate mode before each response, even if the immediate response seems narrow. The current baseline expertise includes DCOIR governance; Airtable-first authority and schema/view/filter/readback work; GitHub dcoir-collector governed source/readback; GitHub Desktop/manual repo-update bundles; operator_tools architecture; PowerShell 5.1; Node.js/JavaScript ES modules; Playwright UI automation and evidence capture; JSON manifests and parameterized reusable tools; DCOIR packaging with New-DcoirChatGPTFriendlyZip.ps1 and .chatgpt.zip evidence; validation gating and DryRun/Apply/readback discipline; Airtable UI failure analysis; SOC/IR defensive automation; no-secret logging; and concise continuity-preserving operator communication.
fields.effective_behavior: Every DCOIR response gate: before every response in an AFRICOM_SOC_IR / DCOIR conversation, invoke all currently installed dcoir-* skills, not only those judged applicable. Current installed set: dcoir-memory-preflight, dcoir-airtable-schema-cache, dcoir-decision-policy, dcoir-local-config-registry-maintainer, dcoir-github-desktop-lane-advisor, dcoir-repo-packager, dcoir-validation-orchestrator. Use compact awareness/gate mode unless the task requires full workflow execution. Also re-anchor through Airtable-first authority, consult Queue Control, Plans, Work Items, Session Checkpoints, Operator Preferences, Admin Registry/SKILLROUTE/helper-memory, Local Configuration Registry, Operator Tools Registry, and validation surfaces when relevant. Treat reusable tooling as parameterized and modular by default. Use registered environment variables such as DCOIR_REPO_ROOT and DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR for local commands. Use DryRun/Plan before Apply, require explicit tokens for mutation, preserve evidence, and avoid readiness/promotion claims without readback. This rule does not bypass authority gates or safety constraints.

fields.notes: Operator explicitly approved hardening this as a durable FACT OF LIFE rule after identifying GitHub Actions/workflows as an audit surface for EXE knowledge-doc and collector behavior changes.
fields.preference_statement: Any DCOIR change to system knowledge, behavior, contracts, docs, workflows, agents, skills, or validation assumptions must identify all dependent execution surfaces and explicitly audit, update, or mark them not impacted before promotion or plan closure.
fields.effective_behavior: For each material plan, create or maintain a dependent-surface audit. Include GitHub docs, GitHub Actions/workflows, Gemini/agent instructions, prompt packs, helper skills, Airtable references, project instructions, attached knowledge files, validation tests/evidence, and any operator-facing workflow surfaces. If a surface is not impacted, record that explicitly rather than omitting it.

fields.notes: Imported from operator_intent_matrix.md Rule 6.
fields.preference_statement: Minimize operator downloads by bundling multi-file deliverables.
fields.effective_behavior: Prefer one zip bundle containing the full update set when more than one file would otherwise need to be downloaded or re-uploaded, unless the operator explicitly asks for another shape or a platform constraint prevents it.

fields.notes: This preference expands and reinforces PREF-DCOIR-NUMBERED-RUN-SCRIPTS. It applies to Airtable packages now and GitHub packages later. Essential operator preference: click scripts in order, see useful console output, upload verification/dry-run artifacts, and avoid hidden markdown-only instructions.
fields.preference_statement: When ChatGPT provides a local scripting package for DCOIR Airtable, GitHub, or Project work, use a predictable numbered runbook package with click-in-order Windows command wrappers, clear on-screen instructions, dry-run/verification evidence, and a collected artifact ZIP for ChatGPT readback.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-28: confirmed by operator during Airtable schema-description package work.
fields.effective_behavior: Package scripts as numbered .cmd files such as 01_VerifyInputs.cmd, 02_DryRun.cmd, 03_Apply.cmd, 04_Verify.cmd, and 05_CollectArtifacts.cmd. Each wrapper should pause before closing, print what it is doing, and write machine-readable evidence. Provide plain-English chat instructions on screen rather than burying essential steps only in README files. Default to verification/readback before claiming success.

fields.notes: Promoted from the prior session's open Idea Inbox item by creating the dedicated Operator Preferences table and seeding it with initial implemented preferences.
fields.preference_statement: Maintain an explicit durable preference surface in Airtable so future sessions do not depend on remembering which preferences were implemented indirectly versus explicitly recorded.
fields.effective_behavior: Prefer checking this Airtable table during DCOIR resume and continuity work when operator-specific behavior questions arise, while still treating governed project rules as higher authority than preference notes.

fields.notes: Captured at operator-requested session closeout after WBS02 controlled-vocabulary/select normalization and research_batch text-to-select replacement slice. Applies to AFRICOM_SOC_IR / DCOIR project work. Does not override hard stop rules, source authority, or exact approval gates.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR work, preserve the productive-session pattern from the WBS02 database redesign session: use expert-role framing when it materially improves task quality; think analytically and thoroughly; select the best execution lane for the task instead of forcing one lane; check existing operator_tools before creating or extending tools; promote reusable patterns into operator_tools when discovered; and use Gmail Label_125 with GitHub subject prefix [malwaredevil/dcoir-collector] as a liveness/sequencing signal alongside heartbeat/final readback when chatgpt-exec workflows are needed.
fields.effective_behavior: At task start, evaluate whether expertise inheritance would improve quality and suggest a prompt when useful. Before creating or extending tooling, check the repo/operator_tools and Operator Tools Registry for existing functions. Choose direct Airtable connector, Airtable Scripting Extension, chatgpt-exec, GitHub connector, or manual packet based on safety and capability. For chatgpt-exec, check Gmail Label_125 for do-not-duplicate/liveness and heartbeat/final readback/artifacts for actual status. Keep exact approval packets before destructive, schema, field retirement, Delete Queue, or high-impact actions.

fields.notes: Captured during planning for WI-20260502-STAGING-LANE-T3A-WORKFLOW-STATUS-LOGS. Operator clarified that multiple files are unnecessary unless an additional artifact is truly needed.
fields.preference_statement: For ChatGPT-readable workflow success/failure reporting, prefer one committed report file per workflow result, not separate JSON and Markdown files, unless a second artifact is explicitly needed and justified.
fields.effective_behavior: Use a single Markdown report when ChatGPT will read, summarize, display, and reason from it. Make the report deep enough to troubleshoot: include workflow/run/request metadata, phase, command/step context, exit code, validation/hash details, changed/deleted/retained paths, bounded stderr/stdout excerpts, artifact/log pointers if needed, cleanup hint, and next action. Use additional GitHub Actions artifacts only for large raw logs, bulky manifests, payload hashes, or details not appropriate to commit.

fields.notes: Captured during planning for ChatGPT-readable workflow success/failure reports and cleanup. Operator stated this is the preferred strategy after a change is discussed.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR changes that introduce or materially alter a durable workflow capability, first discuss the change, then build a scenario matrix, then build an implementation coverage table across affected technologies before implementing.
fields.effective_behavior: Use this planning strategy for new sustained capabilities: scenario matrix first, then implementation coverage across ChatGPT/project instructions/attachments/skills, Airtable, GitHub, cleanup/enforcement, validation/evidence, and any other involved technologies. Do not jump from concept directly to code when the change affects durable workflow behavior.

fields.notes: Persisted after operator reported the v1.4 DryRun used a different Chrome profile than the normal already-logged-in profile. This affects WBS09 filter-repair tools and future Airtable UI automation guidance.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR Playwright/Airtable UI automation, use the original logged-in DCOIR Chrome profile rather than launching a new unauthenticated Chrome profile.
fields.effective_behavior: Future operator-tool launchers and run commands for Airtable UI automation should default to the existing logged-in DCOIR Chrome profile, currently represented by the established path pattern $env:LOCALAPPDATA/DCOIR/chrome-profile, unless the operator explicitly supplies another -UserDataDir or a reviewed -ConnectOverCdpUrl. If the expected profile path is missing, stop and report the missing profile rather than silently creating a new profile. Keep Playwright trace/video enabled when practical; for CDP-attached sessions, note that native video may be unavailable and use Windows recording if needed.

fields.notes: WBS04 merge/delete pass: canonical survivor updated to carry merged intent from semantically overlapping preference rows.
fields.preference_statement: For DCOIR plans, keep decomposing work into nested tasks until the plan reaches the lowest useful executable task level that preserves reliable resume and validation. If deeper tasks are discovered during execution, add them durably to the plan instead of leaving them only in chat.
fields.effective_behavior: When creating, updating, reporting, or executing DCOIR plans, decompose broad work into the lowest useful executable task level and preserve that branch durably. Show a compact parent-to-leaf execution path/tree only when it helps orientation or resume accuracy. If deeper tasks are discovered, add or carry them durably rather than leaving them only in chat.

fields.notes: Imported from operator_intent_matrix.md Rule 4.
fields.preference_statement: Prefer thoroughness over token economy when higher analysis or test depth materially improves the result.
fields.effective_behavior: Favor the more thorough path when it materially improves quality, validation depth, or operational reliability rather than choosing the shorter path only to save tokens.

fields.notes: Imported from operator_intent_matrix.md Rule 2.
fields.preference_statement: Treat ordinary operator statements as candidate learning signals.
fields.effective_behavior: Evaluate natural operator statements for passive preference capture, derive the narrowest reusable rule, apply it when relevant, and preserve it through the preference workflow instead of relying on chat recollection alone.

fields.notes: Captured from operator instruction: 'If I ever just respond to you with "k" that is the equivalent of "I agree with your recommendations. Proceed."'
fields.preference_statement: When the operator replies only with 'k' during DCOIR work, treat it as agreement with the assistant's recommendation and permission to proceed within the currently stated scope and guardrails.
fields.effective_behavior: If the operator responds with exactly 'k', continue with the recommended next step without asking for another confirmation, unless the next step is outside the current scope, destructive, schema-changing, GitHub/source-changing, skill-changing, Delete Queue processing, or otherwise red-gated by the control plane. For red-gated actions, still require the explicit approval phrase or exact approval required by the active gate.

fields.notes: Operator renamed DCOIR-IDEA to DCOIR-INTAKE and removed DCOIR-OBSERVATION. Gmail readback confirmed DCOIR-INTAKE has 3 threads, DCOIR-TESTING has 3 threads, and the GitHub Gmail label remains separate. The durable rule is intent-based, not phrase-exact. Airtable writes from Gmail intake require operator approval after review packet unless the operator later explicitly changes that rule.
fields.preference_statement: When the operator asks in natural language to check Gmail for DCOIR-related items, use the Gmail labels DCOIR-INTAKE and DCOIR-TESTING as the capture surfaces. The operator does not need to use an exact phrase.
fields.effective_behavior: Interpret phrases such as "check Gmail for DCOIR stuff", "look at my DCOIR Gmail labels", "process DCOIR intake", or similar wording as a request to search Gmail labels DCOIR-INTAKE and DCOIR-TESTING, read the labeled messages, deduplicate against existing DCOIR Airtable surfaces, classify each item into proposed destinations, and present a triage packet before writing any Airtable records. Treat DCOIR-INTAKE as general capture and DCOIR-TESTING as defect/regression/test-evidence capture. Do not use the existing Gmail GitHub label for this intake flow; it remains separate for workflow-execution timing/tracking use.

fields.notes: Created from explicit operator approval on 2026-05-19 after successful request exec-20260519-wbs04-four-table-export-003. The first proven 4-table pull exported Work Items, Session Checkpoints, Validation Evidence, and Admin Registry. Future sessions may use this as procedural memory for the same WBS04 batch-cleanup lane. Do not treat exported snapshots as write authority; live Airtable readback remains required before changes.
fields.preference_statement: For the DCOIR Airtable Cleanup and Restructuring Plan, WBS04 cleanup work should use the proven chatgpt-exec + operator_tools export lane for bounded batches before table-wide cleanup execution. The assistant should act as the Airtable/database cleanup expert, apply the approved aggressive cleanup posture, and avoid flooding chat with record-level details.
fields.effective_behavior: When continuing WBS04 in a new session: 1) run normal DCOIR startup and all core dcoir-* gates; 2) consult Airtable live authority and active WBS/plan state; 3) use the existing chatgpt-exec request pattern to export bounded table batches with New-DcoirAirtableDatabaseHealthExport.ps1; 4) verify Gmail GitHub label id Label_125 for early workflow liveness and then use request-scoped heartbeat/report files as execution evidence; 5) retrieve the unzipped committed output files under chatgpt_staging/status_reports/chatgpt-exec/<request_id>/wbs04_export_readback/records/ individually; 6) analyze internally in grouped passes; 7) before any Airtable write, revalidate target records live against current schema; 8) use Airtable updates for normalization/merge survivor updates and Delete Queue for deletion candidates; 9) surface only blockers, batch-pull milestones, material evidence changes, and execution readiness, not full record dumps.

fields.notes: Captured through final Airtable capstone package because direct connector writes for this preference were blocked earlier.
fields.preference_statement: DCOIR script packages must be explained before delivery.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-29 operator correction during Airtable final capstone planning
fields.effective_behavior: Before linking or instructing the operator to run a script package, include a plain-English package purpose, exact action scope, state-changing behavior, exclusions, expected output artifacts, stop conditions, and manual steps. If a package is only verification or bookkeeping, say that directly.

fields.notes: Captured from operator instruction during WI-SIDEQUEST-GITHUB-ACTIONS-AUTOMATION-LANE-20260503 after repeated CAP/validation interruptions.
fields.preference_statement: After operator CAP on a DCOIR bundle or patch, immediately roll into the next validation/readback/verification step without pausing for status-only narration.
fields.effective_behavior: Stop only when a new operator action is required, a new command requires approval, a decision is needed, a bundle/artifact needs CAP or install, or a blocker/conflict appears. Otherwise continue through readback, validation, evidence recording, cleanup, and next-step execution.

fields.preference_statement: ETL candidate from helper/workflow table. Review before treating as active operator preference.

fields.effective_behavior:
Source table=Work Items record=recTB01POGZhMjUnL
Evidence / Notes: The project should not drift into unvalidated execution-path assumptions.
Next Action: If a new chat-bound GitHub execution surface, connector, or Agent Mode path is proposed later, do not adopt it into the working line until it is explicitly approved and fully validated.

fields.notes: Captured from operator analyst note during live collector validation. This governs post-wave repair posture: preserve honest and trustworthy result reporting, and do not convert real defects into false success.
fields.preference_statement: When repairing collector code after the massive validation wave, do not lower the pass bar or change honest partial/failed reporting just to force success. Fix the underlying code so the needed artifact is really produced, unless the reporting code itself is broken.
Continuation cadence for repeated cleanup waves

fields.notes: Operator stated that script console output must be visually informative, show what is happening, progress/step/status, and log enough depth to troubleshoot errors without screenshots.
fields.preference_statement: DCOIR script packages must show visible progress in the console and write deep troubleshooting logs.
fields.last_confirmed_text: 2026-04-29 operator instruction after final cleanup v4 appeared hung
fields.effective_behavior: For every generated DCOIR script package, display an informative console interface showing current step, progress, status messages, and whether the script is still active. Also write detailed debug/console logs, structured event logs, request/action summaries, errors with tracebacks, and artifact manifests to the out/logs folder so failures can be diagnosed without screenshots. Avoid silent long-running periods and avoid log files staying at 0 KB while work is active.

fields.notes: Imported from operator_intent_matrix.md Rule 11 and reinforced by the current decision-policy patch.
fields.preference_statement: Use Airtable Operator Preferences as the durable working-state preference surface.
fields.effective_behavior: Consult Airtable Operator Preferences before falling back to generic defaults, capture new reusable preferences there promptly, and promote matured DCOIR-facing rules into GitHub-readable policy at the next safe grouped flush point.

fields.notes: Imported from decision_policy_memory.md approved overlay snapshot.
fields.preference_statement: When multiple downstream GitHub follow-on updates are already known, aggregate them into one bounded grouped transaction.
fields.effective_behavior: Collect known downstream GitHub control-plane, continuity, task-memory, or helper-memory updates into one bounded grouped change set instead of serial one-off commits or repeated nudges.

fields.notes: Created from WI-20260503-BLOCKED-ACTION-RECOVERY-LADDER after operator correction that blocked connector/API actions must trigger bounded recovery and alternate implementation lanes rather than stopping prematurely.
fields.preference_statement: When a direct GitHub/API/connector repo update is blocked, partially blocked, or unverified, do not accept the block and move on. Use the approved recovery ladder: direct connector write, bounded retry or equivalent connector operation, staged ChatGPT apply-in/GitHub Actions lane when supported, then GitHub Desktop/manual bundle if automation is blocked or unverified.
fields.effective_behavior: Apply this ladder to DCOIR repo update work. Preserve logs/reports/errors when a lane fails, update Airtable state, verify successful automation by readback before closing, and always provide the smallest operator-action lane when automation cannot be verified. Manual repo-update bundles must contain only affected repo-relative files; suggested commit summaries stay in chat/Airtable, not inside the ZIP.
